---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## 

```{r}
# Make sure you have these libraries installed
# install.packages("ggplot2")
# install.packages("plotly")
# install.packages("dplyr")

census_api_key("29a0b950ad6549f8dc16707914d212045d3b5f3a", install = TRUE)


library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)

# --- 1. Load Map Data ---
# ggplot2 comes with built-in map data for states and counties
county_map_data <- map_data("county")
state_map_data <- map_data("state")

# --- 2. Recreate your sample location data ---
# (Using sample data for a reproducible example)
p_location_data2 <- data.frame(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  Company = c("3M", "DuPont", "3M", "Chemours", "DuPont"),
  lat = c(34.20, 39.65, 35.10, 34.82, 33.74),
  lon = c(-84.01, -75.66, -89.97, -79.33, -84.38)
)

# --- 3. Build the map with ggplot2 ---
p <- ggplot() +
  # Add county boundaries (thin, light gray lines)
  geom_polygon(
    data = county_map_data,
    aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
    fill = "gray95", # Light fill for counties
    color = "white"   # White lines between counties
  ) +
  # Add state boundaries (thicker, darker lines)
  geom_polygon(
    data = state_map_data,
    aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
    fill = NA, # No fill
    color = "gray60", # Darker line for states
    linewidth = 0.6 # Use linewidth instead of size for polygons
  ) +
  # Add your location markers on top
  geom_point(
    data = p_location_data2,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, color = Company,
        # Custom data for the hover text
        text = paste("Company:", Company)),
    size = 3,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  # Use an appropriate map projection
  coord_map("albers", lat0 = 39, lat1 = 45) +
  # Clean up the theme for a map-like appearance
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right") # Adjust legend position

# --- 4. Convert the ggplot object to an interactive plotly object ---
# Use the tooltip argument to specify what to show on hover
ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("text"))



```

```{r}
# Make sure you have these libraries installed
# install.packages("ggplot2")
# install.packages("plotly")
# install.packages("dplyr")
# install.packages("tidycensus")
# install.packages("sf")

#census_api_key("29a0b950ad6549f8dc16707914d212045d3b5f3a", install = TRUE)

library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tidycensus)
library(sf)

# --- 1. Get Population Density Data from Census ---
county_density <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = "B01003_001",  # Total population variable
  year = 2022,
  survey = "acs5",
  geometry = TRUE
) %>%
  rename(population = estimate) %>%
  # Calculate population density (people per square mile)
  mutate(
    area_sq_miles = as.numeric(st_area(geometry)) / 2589988.11, # Convert from sq meters to sq miles
    density = population / area_sq_miles
  ) %>%
  # Convert to same coordinate system as map_data
  st_transform(crs = 4326) %>%
  # Extract centroid coordinates for plotting
  mutate(
    long = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geometry))[,1],
    lat = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geometry))[,2]
  )

# --- 2. Load Map Data ---
# ggplot2 comes with built-in map data for states and counties
county_map_data <- map_data("county")
state_map_data <- map_data("state")

# --- 3. Recreate your sample location data ---
p_location_data2 <- data.frame(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  Company = c("3M", "DuPont", "3M", "Chemours", "DuPont"),
  lat = c(34.20, 39.65, 35.10, 34.82, 33.74),
  lon = c(-84.01, -75.66, -89.97, -79.33, -84.38)
)

# --- 4. Build the map with ggplot2 ---
p <- ggplot() +
  # Add county density as filled polygons
  geom_sf(
    data = county_density,
    aes(fill = density, 
        text = paste(NAME, "<br>Density:", round(density, 1), "people/sq mi")),
    color = "white",
    size = 0.2
  ) +
  # Add state boundaries (thicker, darker lines)
  geom_polygon(
    data = state_map_data,
    aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
    fill = NA,
    color = "gray40",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  # Add your location markers on top
  geom_point(
    data = p_location_data2,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, color = Company,
        text = paste("Company:", Company, "<br>Lat:", lat, "<br>Lon:", lon)),
    size = 3,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  # Color scale for population density
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "Population Density\n(people/sq mi)",
    option = "plasma",
    trans = "log10",  # Log scale for better visualization
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  # Color scale for companies
  scale_color_brewer(name = "Company", palette = "Set1") +
  # Clean up the theme for a map-like appearance
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

# --- 5. Convert the ggplot object to an interactive plotly object ---
ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("text")) %>%
  layout(
    title = "Company Locations and Population Density by County"
  )
```
